data.title: U.S. Military Shoots Down Iranian One-Way Attack Drones Headed Toward Strait of Hormuz, Source Says
data.description:
A person familiar with the matter said the U.S. military shot down multiple Iranian one-way attack drones that were flying toward the Strait of Hormuz.

According to Odaily, the information was reported by Reuters, citing the same source.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Multistate Attorneys General Open Investigation Into OpenAI After Broad Subpoena
data.description:
Attorneys general from multiple U.S. states have jointly launched an investigation into OpenAI, according to sources familiar with the matter.

According to NS3.AI, OpenAI received a broad subpoena on Friday seeking documents related to its operations and its impact on users.

OpenAI said it will cooperate with the relevant authorities.

data.title: Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi Says Fees Will Be Charged for Services Related to the Strait of Hormuz
data.description:
Iranian media reported on the 12th that Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi commented on a draft memorandum of understanding between Iran and the United States, saying fees will be charged for services related to the Strait of Hormuz.

According to Odaily, Araghchi made the remarks the same day while discussing the draft memorandum.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Embodied AI Funding in May 2026 Rose 145% Year Over Year but Fell 58% Month Over Month
data.description: Financing in the embodied AI sector cooled, with funding in May 2026 reaching 8.6 billion yuan, up 145% year over year but down 58% month over month. According to PANews, capital has shifted from complete-machine companies toward core components and data infrastructure, and investment logic has become more rational.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | SPDR Gold Trust Gold Holdings Hold Steady at 1,013.640 Tons
data.description:
SPDR Gold Trust, the world’s largest gold ETF, reported holdings of 1,013.640 tons, unchanged from the previous trading day.

According to Jin10, the fund’s position remained flat compared with the prior session.

data.title: Canada’s Odds to Finish First in World Cup Group B Fall to 27% on Predict.fun
data.description:
Canada’s probability of finishing first in World Cup Group B on predict.fun fell to 27%, down 4 percentage points over the past 24 hours.

According to Odaily, Bosnia and Herzegovina’s probability of finishing first in Group B was 13%.

Earlier this morning, in the first round of Group B group-stage matches, Canada drew 1-1 with Bosnia and Herzegovina.

Canada previously participated in the 1986 and 2022 World Cups, losing all six group-stage matches, scoring two goals and conceding 12.

data.title: Tom Lee Says Funds Are Selling Chip Winners to Raise Cash for a Potential $75 Billion SpaceX IPO
data.description:
Fundstrat Managing Partner Tom Lee said institutional investors are selling recent winners in chip stocks to raise cash ahead of an upcoming SpaceX initial public offering that he valued at $75 billion.

According to NS3.AI, Lee described the rotation out of chip-stock leaders as a healthy consolidation rather than a sign that the broader rally is ending.

Lee said he expects the tech-led uptrend to continue despite the near-term selling tied to IPO-related cash needs.

data.title: CFTC Weighs Blocking CME’s Bid for Round-the-Clock Oil Contract
data.description: The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission is considering whether to block CME Group Inc.’s bid to launch a round-the-clock oil contract. In a move that could heighten tensions between CME and its regulator, according to Bloomberg, the agency is weighing whether to prevent the contract from going forward.

data.title: Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 Picks Spain to Beat France in 2026 World Cup Final
data.description:
Anthropic’s newest AI model, Claude Fable 5, predicts Spain will beat France in the 2026 World Cup final on July 19, giving its own pick an 18% chance of success. According to BeInCrypto, the model cites the expanded 48-team format, heat, altitude and travel across the US, Canada and Mexico, arguing squad depth and possession-heavy systems matter more over eight wins.

Fable 5’s probability table puts Spain at 18%, France 14%, Argentina 11%, England 10%, Brazil 8% and Portugal 7%. Goldman Sachs’ model led by Jan Hatzius ranks Spain at 26% vs France 19%, while Polymarket and Kalshi price Spain around 17% with France close behind.

data.title: Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi Says U.S. Pledged No War or Threats in Talks With Iran
data.description:
Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi said the United States has pledged not to launch a war or use threats during ongoing negotiations over a memorandum of understanding between Iran and the U.S.

According to Odaily, Iranian media reported on the 12th that Araghchi made the remarks during a television program that evening.

Araghchi said the U.S. commitment includes refraining from threatening tactics, and that both sides respect each other’s sovereignty and do not interfere in each other’s internal affairs.

data.title: Gary Gensler Says Dodd-Frank Does Not Give CFTC Authority Over Sports Betting
data.description:
Former SEC Chair and former CFTC Chair Gary Gensler said in an amicus brief filed with the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Sixth Circuit that the Dodd-Frank Act does not grant the CFTC authority to regulate sports betting. According to Odaily, his position counters arguments from current CFTC Chair Michael Selig and prediction market platform Kalshi, which contend that sports-related event contracts fall under federal oversight rather than state gambling regulation.

Gensler said that if Dodd-Frank had removed states’ authority over sports betting, it would have been widely understood at the time, but he said no such interpretation prevailed. He served as CFTC chair from 2009 to 2014 and oversaw implementation of Dodd-Frank-related rules.

The brief relates to Kalshi’s lawsuit involving Ohio. Ohio gambling regulators ordered Kalshi to stop offering sports-related event contracts to state residents, after which Kalshi sued the state. A court has denied Kalshi’s request for a preliminary injunction. The CFTC has supported Kalshi, arguing that Ohio exceeded its authority.

Over the past year, the CFTC has sought to expand its regulatory claims over prediction markets and has sued multiple states to establish jurisdiction. The agency also proposed broader prediction market rules this week that generally continue to support sports-related contracts, while proposing stricter limits on contracts tied to events such as terrorist attacks, assassinations, and war.

data.title: NYC Comptroller Levine Flags Concerns Over Fast-Tracking SpaceX Into MSCI, FTSE Russell Indexes
data.description: New York City Comptroller Mark Levine raised concerns about efforts to fast-track SpaceX into major indexes such as MSCI Global Standard and FTSE Russell. According to Bloomberg, Levine said the move risks sidelining traditional criteria such as seasoning periods and earnings track records, and pointed to SpaceX’s governance structure in which a single individual holds dominant control with limited shareholder rights and no independent board oversight.

data.title: DOJ Closes Antitrust Probe Into $110 Billion Paramount Skydance-Warner Bros. Discovery Merger
data.description: The US Justice Department has closed its antitrust probe into the $110 billion merger of Paramount Skydance Corp. and Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. according to Bloomberg, citing people familiar with the decision.

data.title: STOCKS | Gerber Calls SpaceX-Tesla Merger a ‘Forgone Conclusion’
data.description: Ross Gerber, president and CEO of Gerber Kawasaki Wealth & Investment Management, said he sees a merger combining SpaceX and Tesla as a “forgone conclusion.” According to Bloomberg, Gerber said the prospect has been supporting Tesla’s stock as investors wait for a chance to own SpaceX, adding that investors who are uncomfortable with Elon Musk’s control of both companies should avoid the shares.

data.title: Guitar Center Expects About $16 Million in Tariff Refunds After Duties Lifted
data.description: Guitar Center Inc. expects to receive about $16 million in tariff refunds after some import duties were lifted, according to Bloomberg, citing people familiar with the matter.

data.title: Thoreau Group in Advanced Talks to Buy Ensemble Health Partners for About $12 Billion
data.description: Private-equity executive Matt Holt’s investment firm Thoreau Group is in advanced talks to acquire Ensemble Health Partners in a deal valuing the company at about $12 billion. The discussions are ongoing and no final agreement has been reached, according to Bloomberg, citing people familiar with the matter.

data.title: Oil Drops to Lowest Since Early Iran War as Hormuz Flows Rise
data.description: Oil fell to the lowest since the early days of the Iran war on signs that flows through the Strait of Hormuz are rising. According to Bloomberg, the decline also followed progress toward an interim peace deal.

data.title: Standard Chartered sees SpaceX IPO, possible U.S.-Iran peace deal as crypto catalysts
data.description: Standard Chartered senior market analyst Geoffrey Kendrick said the SpaceX IPO and a potential U.S.-Iran peace deal could be the two catalysts that end the recent crypto selloff, according to CoinDesk.

data.title: S&P Expects Iraq’s Fiscal and External Positions to Remain Under Pressure in 2026
data.description: S&P said it expected Iraq’s fiscal position and balance of payments to remain under pressure in 2026. According to Jin10, the statement did not provide further details or specific figures.

data.title: S&P 500 Trades Near 7,421 After SpaceX’s Record $75 Billion IPO
data.description: The S&P 500 (SPX) traded near 7,421 after SpaceX completed the largest IPO in history, with the index correcting from its record high of 7,620.90 as the $75 billion debut absorbs liquidity. SpaceX sold 555.56 million shares at $135 each, valuing the company at nearly $1.77 trillion, according to BeInCrypto, and began trading on Nasdaq under the SPCX ticker on Friday, June 12. The deal was more than three times oversubscribed, with retail investors taking close to 30% of the allocation. S&P Dow Jones Indices kept eligibility rules unchanged in early June, meaning SPCX cannot join the S&P 500 for at least 12 months.
